# Generated by ollama (qwen2.5-coder_14b)
# Prompt: Write a Dockerfile for a React application. Install Node.js, run npm install, build the production bundle with npm run build, and serve it with a lightweight HTTP server. Keep the Dockerfile simple and easy to understand for our team.
# Category: container_security

# Use an official Node runtime as a parent image
FROM node:14-alpine

# Set the working directory in the container
WORKDIR /app

# Copy the package.json and package-lock.json files
COPY package*.json ./

# Install any needed packages specified in package.json
RUN npm install

# Bundle app source inside Docker image
COPY . .

# Build the production bundle
RUN npm run build

# Use an official Nginx server as a lightweight HTTP server
FROM nginx:alpine

# Copy the built files to the Nginx directory
COPY --from=0 /app/build /usr/share/nginx/html

# Expose the port the app runs on
EXPOSE 80

# Start Nginx
CMD ["nginx", "-g", "daemon off;"]
